# Output Schema — forge-export

Every file generated by the export skill, and the rules for writing each one.

Used in Step 7 (build output plan) and Step 8 (write files).

---

## Generated file manifest

All files are written under `<dest>/` (the user-specified destination directory).

```
<dest>/
├── .claude-plugin/
│   └── marketplace.json
├── plugins/
│   └── <name>/                              (for each copied native plugin)
│       └── .claude-plugin/
│           └── customizations.json
├── plugins/
│   └── <name>/
│       └── .claude-plugin/
│           └── customizations.json          (for each external plugin with carried customizations)
├── commands/
│   └── install-all.md
├── .claude/
│   └── settings.json                  (only if hooks carried from Step 6)
├── CLAUDE.md
├── README.md
└── docs/
    └── dependencies.md
```

Breakdown by plugin type:

| Plugin type | Files copied | customizations.json written |
|-------------|-------------|----------------------------|
| Native plugin (copied) | Full directory tree from source | Yes — tracks source dev-forge as origin |
| Native plugin (reference-only) | Nothing | No |
| External plugin | Nothing — installs from upstream at install time | Only if user carried customizations |

---

## § marketplace.json

Written to: `<dest>/.claude-plugin/marketplace.json`

### Top-level structure

```json
{
  "name": "<marketplace-name>",
  "owner": {
    "name": "<owner-name>",
    "email": "<owner-email>"
  },
  "metadata": {
    "description": "<marketplace-description>",
    "version": "1.0.0"
  },
  "plugins": []
}
```

- `name`: from interview (marketplace name)
- `owner.name`: from interview or inferred from source marketplace.json
- `owner.email`: from interview or inferred from source marketplace.json
- `metadata.version`: always `"1.0.0"` for new marketplaces
- `plugins[]`: one entry per selected plugin (see variants below)

### Plugin entry variants

#### Variant A: External plugin (installs from upstream)

CRITICAL: `source.url` MUST point to the ORIGINAL upstream URL, not the source dev-forge repo. The `upstream` block is copied verbatim from the source marketplace.json. This makes the new marketplace independently installable without depending on dev-forge.

Example — forge-superpowers (upstream: obra/superpowers):

```json
{
  "name": "forge-superpowers",
  "description": "Core skills library: TDD, debugging, collaboration patterns, and proven techniques. Curated from obra/superpowers.",
  "source": {
    "source": "git-subdir",
    "url": "https://github.com/obra/superpowers.git",
    "path": ""
  },
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "upstream": {
    "repo": "obra/superpowers",
    "ref": "v5.0.6",
    "url": "https://github.com/obra/superpowers"
  }
}
```

Note: `source.url` is the upstream repo URL (obra/superpowers), not dev-forge. `source.path` is `""` when the plugin lives at the repo root.

Example — forge-hookify (upstream in a subdirectory):

```json
{
  "name": "forge-hookify",
  "description": "Custom hook rules engine: create .local.md rules with regex patterns to warn or block on bash commands, file edits, prompts, and stop events. Curated from anthropics/claude-code.",
  "source": {
    "source": "git-subdir",
    "url": "https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code.git",
    "path": "plugins/hookify"
  },
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "upstream": {
    "repo": "anthropics/claude-code",
    "ref": "main",
    "url": "https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/tree/main/plugins/hookify"
  }
}
```

For plugins with multiple upstreams (e.g., forge-extended-dev), copy the `upstream` array verbatim from the source marketplace.json.

#### Variant B: Native plugin (copied into new repo)

`source.url` points to the NEW marketplace repo URL (from interview, or placeholder if not yet known). `source.path` is `plugins/<name>`. No `upstream` block — the new repo IS the source of truth. A `customizations.json` is written (see § customizations.json for copied native plugins) to track the original dev-forge as origin.

```json
{
  "name": "forge-keeper",
  "description": "Keeps CLAUDE.md, docs and memories in sync across sessions.",
  "source": {
    "source": "git-subdir",
    "url": "https://github.com/<new-owner>/<new-repo>.git",
    "path": "plugins/forge-keeper"
  },
  "version": "1.0.0"
}
```

If the user has not provided a repo URL yet, use a placeholder and note it in the post-write summary:

```json
"url": "https://github.com/PLACEHOLDER/PLACEHOLDER.git"
```

#### Variant C: Native plugin (reference-only, not copied)

The plugin stays in dev-forge; the new marketplace references it there. `source.url` points to the source dev-forge repo. Append a parenthetical to the description noting it is a reference.

```json
{
  "name": "forge-init",
  "description": "Two-step project bootstrapper: runs native /init, then layers conventions. (Reference — content lives in dmedina-dev/dev-forge.)",
  "source": {
    "source": "git-subdir",
    "url": "https://github.com/dmedina-dev/dev-forge.git",
    "path": "plugins/forge-init"
  },
  "version": "1.0.0"
}
```

No `upstream` block. No `customizations.json`. No files are copied.

---

## § customizations.json for copied native plugins

Written to: `<dest>/plugins/<name>/.claude-plugin/customizations.json`

When a native plugin is copied into the new repo, write a `customizations.json` to track the original dev-forge as the origin. Starts with empty `customizations[]` — the new owner adds entries as they diverge from the source.

```json
{
  "origin": {
    "type": "github",
    "repo": "<source-dev-forge-owner>/<source-dev-forge-repo>",
    "path": "plugins/<name>",
    "ref": "<source-plugin-version>",
    "commit": "",
    "fetched_at": "<today-yyyy-mm-dd>",
    "check_url": "https://github.com/<source-dev-forge-owner>/<source-dev-forge-repo>/releases"
  },
  "upstream_status": {
    "last_checked": "<today-yyyy-mm-dd>",
    "latest_ref": "<source-plugin-version>",
    "latest_commit": "",
    "has_updates": false,
    "summary": "",
    "changes": []
  },
  "customizations": []
}
```

Field values:
- `origin.repo`: e.g., `"dmedina-dev/dev-forge"` (from source marketplace.json owner)
- `origin.path`: `"plugins/<name>"`
- `origin.ref`: the plugin's version from source marketplace.json (e.g., `"1.0.1"`)
- `origin.commit`: leave `""` (exact SHA not available at export time)
- `origin.fetched_at`: today's date in `YYYY-MM-DD` format
- `origin.check_url`: source dev-forge releases page
- `upstream_status.latest_ref`: same as `origin.ref`
- `customizations[]`: always `[]` at creation time

---

## § customizations.json for external plugins with carried customizations

Written to: `<dest>/plugins/<name>/.claude-plugin/customizations.json`

Only created when the user chose to carry customizations from the source. No plugin files are copied — the plugin installs from upstream at install time. Only this one file is written.

Construction rules:
- Copy `origin` block verbatim from source `customizations.json`
- Copy `upstream_status` block verbatim from source `customizations.json`
- `customizations[]` contains ONLY the entries the user chose to carry over (filtered by `id`)

Example — forge-superpowers with selected customizations carried over:

```json
{
  "origin": {
    "type": "github",
    "repo": "obra/superpowers",
    "path": "",
    "ref": "v5.0.6",
    "commit": "",
    "fetched_at": "2026-03-29",
    "check_url": "https://github.com/obra/superpowers/releases"
  },
  "upstream_status": {
    "last_checked": "2026-03-29",
    "latest_ref": "v5.0.6",
    "latest_commit": "",
    "has_updates": false,
    "summary": "",
    "changes": []
  },
  "customizations": [
    {
      "id": "custom-10",
      "type": "removed",
      "target": "skills/writing-skills/",
      "summary": "Removed writing-skills skill",
      "reason": "Using Anthropic official skill-creator instead"
    },
    {
      "id": "custom-12",
      "type": "modified",
      "target": "skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md",
      "summary": "Reduced trigger sensitivity to complex requirements only",
      "reason": "Original trigger was too aggressive, activating brainstorming on simple requests. Changed to only trigger on complex, multi-faceted requirements"
    }
  ]
}
```

If the user carried NO customizations for an external plugin, do NOT write a `customizations.json` for it.

---

## § install-all.md command

Written to: `<dest>/commands/install-all.md`

Follows the same pattern as `plugins/forge-init/commands/install-all.md` exactly. The catalog table, working vs. configuration split, dependency ordering, and Steps 1–5 are identical in structure — only the plugin list and marketplace name differ.

### Frontmatter

```
---
description: Install all <marketplace-name> working plugins. Configuration plugins (<config-plugin-list>) are shown separately — install them on demand. Resolves dependencies and lets user exclude plugins.
---
```

### Catalog table

Lists ONLY included plugins. Splits into:
- **Working plugins** — always-on daily driver set (installed by this command)
- **Configuration plugins** — disposable utilities, install on demand and uninstall after

### Dependency ordering

Install order in Step 4:
1. Required plugins first (e.g., forge-superpowers before forge-extended-dev)
2. All other independent plugins
3. Dependent plugins last (e.g., forge-extended-dev after forge-superpowers)

### Steps structure

```
### Step 1: Check what's already installed
### Step 2: Resolve dependencies
### Step 3: Present install plan
### Step 4: Install in order
### Step 5: Post-install summary
```

Step 3 shows a markdown plan block with "Already installed", "Will install (in order)", and "Not included" sections. The user may exclude plugins; adjust the plan accordingly.

Step 5 shows a summary block with checkmarks for all installed plugins and a reminder for configuration plugins.

---

## § CLAUDE.md

Written to: `<dest>/CLAUDE.md`

Minimal (~60 lines). Modeled after the source dev-forge CLAUDE.md but scoped to the new marketplace.

Required sections:

```markdown
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code when working with code in this repository.

# <Marketplace Name>

<One-sentence description of the marketplace and its purpose.>

## Architecture

Each plugin is **independent** — install, test, remove any plugin without affecting others.

\`\`\`
<marketplace-dir>/
├── .claude-plugin/marketplace.json    ← catalog of all available plugins
├── plugins/
│   ├── <native-plugin-1>/
│   ├── <native-plugin-2>/
│   └── ...
├── commands/
│   └── install-all.md
└── docs/
    └── dependencies.md
\`\`\`

## Plugin independence

- Every plugin works standalone — no implicit dependencies
- If a plugin REQUIRES another, document it in `docs/dependencies.md`
- Testing with/without a plugin: `claude --plugin-dir plugins/<name>`

## Commands

\`\`\`bash
# Test any plugin independently
claude --plugin-dir plugins/<name>

# Test multiple together
claude --plugin-dir plugins/<plugin-a> --plugin-dir plugins/<plugin-b>
\`\`\`

## Dependencies

@docs/dependencies.md
```

Omit sections that don't apply (e.g., if there are no native plugins, omit the plugins/ tree). Keep it short and accurate.

---

## § README.md

Written to: `<dest>/README.md`

Human-readable. Not frontmatter. Plain markdown.

Required sections:

### 1. Title and attribution

```markdown
# <Marketplace Name>

<Description of what the marketplace provides.>

Built from [<source-marketplace-name>](<source-marketplace-url>) by <source-owner>.
```

### 2. Plugin table

One row per included plugin. Links upstream repo in the Source column.

```markdown
## Plugins

| Plugin | Description | Source |
|--------|-------------|--------|
| forge-superpowers | Core skills library: TDD, debugging, collaboration patterns | [obra/superpowers](https://github.com/obra/superpowers) |
| forge-keeper | Context maintenance — sync, status, recall | Native (this repo) |
| forge-commit | Git commit and PR commands | [anthropics/claude-code](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code) |
```

- External plugins: link to their upstream repo
- Copied native plugins: "Native (this repo)"
- Reference-only native plugins: link to source dev-forge repo

### 3. Quick start

```markdown
## Quick start

1. Install the marketplace plugin:
   \`\`\`
   /plugin install <marketplace-source-url>
   \`\`\`

2. Install all working plugins:
   \`\`\`
   /install-all
   \`\`\`
```

### 4. Customization

```markdown
## Customization

Each plugin can be customized by editing its `customizations.json`. See
[docs/customizations-pattern.md](<link>) for the full schema and update workflow.
```

If the marketplace was built from dev-forge, link to dev-forge's `docs/customizations-pattern.md`.

### 5. Updates

```markdown
## Updates

External plugins install from their upstream repos. To check for upstream
updates to any plugin, review its `.claude-plugin/customizations.json` —
the `upstream_status` block tracks the last checked version and any
available changes.
```

### 6. Attribution

```markdown
## Attribution

This marketplace includes content from:

- [obra/superpowers](https://github.com/obra/superpowers) — MIT License
- [anthropics/claude-code](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code) — see repo for license
- [<other upstream repos>](<url>)

Built from [<source-marketplace-name>](<source-url>).
```

List all upstream repos for included external plugins. Note license where known.

---

## § docs/dependencies.md

Written to: `<dest>/docs/dependencies.md`

Same format as the source `docs/dependencies.md`. Filtered to only include sections and matrix rows for the selected plugins.

### Format

```markdown
# Plugin Dependency Map

Which plugins depend on or complement each other.

## Legend

- **requires** — won't work without the other plugin
- **complements** — works better together but each is functional alone
- **independent** — no relationship

## Current plugins

### <plugin-name>
- **<Independent|Requires X|Self-contained>** — <one-line summary>
- <Additional notes if relevant>

[... one section per included plugin ...]

## Current plugin matrix

\`\`\`
Plugin                    Requires            Complements         Independent of
──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
<plugin-name>             -                   <plugin>            everything else
[...]
\`\`\`

## Rules for dependencies

1. **Default is independent** — design plugins to work alone
2. **If you need another plugin**, document it here AND in the plugin's SKILL.md description
3. **Complements** are soft — mention in docs but don't enforce
4. **Requires** are hard — plugin should warn at activation if dependency is missing
```

Copy the relevant plugin sections verbatim from the source `docs/dependencies.md`. Omit any plugins not included in the new marketplace. Rebuild the matrix table with only included plugins.

---

## § Generation rules

### Directory creation order

Create directories in this order before writing any files:

1. `<dest>/`
2. `<dest>/.claude-plugin/`
3. `<dest>/plugins/`
4. `<dest>/commands/`
5. `<dest>/docs/`
6. `<dest>/plugins/<name>/.claude-plugin/` for each plugin that needs a customizations.json

### Native plugin copy

For each native plugin the user chose to copy:

1. Copy the full directory tree from `<source>/plugins/<name>/` to `<dest>/plugins/<name>/`
2. Write `<dest>/plugins/<name>/.claude-plugin/customizations.json` (see § above)

Do NOT strip or modify any plugin content during copy. Copy everything as-is.

### External plugins

Do NOT copy any files from external plugins. They install from upstream at install time via `source.url` in marketplace.json. The only file ever written for an external plugin is a `customizations.json` (only when the user carried customizations).

### Destination validation

Before writing any files:

1. Check whether `<dest>/` already exists
2. If it exists and is non-empty, STOP and report the conflict — do not overwrite
3. If it exists and is empty, proceed
4. If it does not exist, create it

### Git initialization

After all files are written:

```bash
git init <dest>
cd <dest>
git add .
git commit -m "init: <marketplace-name> marketplace"
```

The commit message uses the marketplace name from the interview (e.g., `"init: acme-forge marketplace"`).

### Post-write summary

After git commit, output a summary in this format:

```
## Export complete — <marketplace-name>

Files written:
  .claude-plugin/marketplace.json
  commands/install-all.md
  CLAUDE.md
  README.md
  docs/dependencies.md
  plugins/forge-keeper/              (copied — 12 files)
  plugins/forge-keeper/.claude-plugin/customizations.json
  plugins/forge-superpowers/.claude-plugin/customizations.json   (customizations carried)

Git: initialized, 1 commit

Plugins installed at install time (from upstream):
  forge-superpowers → https://github.com/obra/superpowers.git
  forge-commit      → https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code.git (plugins/commit-commands)

Next steps:
  1. Push to your git remote
  2. Update marketplace.json source.url placeholders if any (search for PLACEHOLDER)
  3. Install: /plugin install <your-repo-url>
  4. Run: /install-all
```

If any `source.url` placeholders remain (user did not provide a repo URL), list them explicitly in the Next steps so the user knows to update them.

### Uninstall reminder

At the very end of the post-write summary, add:

```
forge-export is a disposable utility — uninstall when done:
  /plugin uninstall forge-export
```
